title: Ranunculus muonioensis
intro: Ranunculus muonioensis, also known as Arctic buttercup, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The Arctic buttercup has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Ranunculus muonioensis include infusions and teas, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, although caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity and potential side effects.
